item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
A thought: with all the antagonism between Wondertainment and The Factory, you need to wonder why the unscrupulous Factory hasn't tried to take out Dr. Wondertainment himself.

Answer: they did.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Nitpicks
– hide block

Issues that I saw but didn't influence my voting:

  • "SCP-2933" is likely the designation given to the entity and its containment, but this is never explicitly stated in the description.
  • The D-class is too nonchalant about their mortality, imo. The character works because of their inversion of the D-class trope, but this is a bit much in the other direction and breaks immersion, if only momentarily.
  • I'm not sure why extraneous, personal conversation would be "EXPUNGED" and not just "removed for brevity."

The characterization here is so well done, SCP-2933-A breaks my rusted little heart. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reading that log made me feel kind of sad for Mr Smiles, although the audio file gave me the chills. Whoever made that really did a good job of making it sound creepy, like some deranged person crying out in the darkness.

What sealed the +1 for me here is the skip's dialogue.

I didn't r-really expect everything to hurt this badly, to tell you the truth, but… my father is out there making smiles right now and he'll come back for me someday soon and we'll be together again, just you wait!

Damn son.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
With the pack on this one - a serviceable SCP with a great log. Having caught up on the tales for the Misters now (dunno how it took me so long) I'm mostly glad this ties off one of the points with Wondertainment and implies something with the tensions with the Factory.

But just that totally badass stoicism set(D) off with a desperate hope(Smiles) is so bittersweet.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is quite honestly my favorite SCP ever. I want to go save the poor guy and hug him even if I’d catch the rust disease that’s killing/corrupting/etc him. His pained cries in the audio are legitimately hard to listen to.

While it’s not quite clear exactly what happened here, my own idea is that Dr Wondertainment used to work at the Factory, caught some horrible contagion from there, and passed it off to one of the Misters.

If the rust isn’t fatal, is that D-class still kicking it down there with him? At least poor Mr. Scary might have some company then.

item.title: Regarding the recent page revision
item.content:encoded: Just an FYI, I went ahead and changed the audio player from Flash to HTML5, as Flash is rapidly losing support and already can't be viewed by many people, including Firefox and Mobile users. No content changes were made, and the article is otherwise unchanged.

item.title: Nice with the class D personnel
item.content:encoded: It at first felt like just a neutral vote, but the class D with personality is what really gave me a +1, also how 2933-A was too. When they make it so that both the anomalies and the class D have personalities and feelings is what gives me a good feeling.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am confused. Is this a collab between Dr. Wondertainment and The Factory? If not, then why are the two both here here?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why do all the skips created to do good always have the worst [EXPLETIVE REDACTED] happen to them?! The crying in the audio log just broke my heart… +1 I guess….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP itself is meh, but you have my upvote for D-133211. Every skip with nonretarded, nonredshirt D class personnel deserves an upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have to say, the concept here didn't really impress me all that much. The characterization, however, was excellent. D-133211 came off as a really decent guy honestly - the kind of guy who didn't really want to cause any trouble or hurt anybody, and I honestly felt really sorry for him in the end. Also, props for breaking the trope of recalcitrant D-class. I also really appreciated the "I'm sorry" from the Foundation controller. That really served to humanize him a fair bit - honestly, it's what I probably would have said had I been in his situation. And finally, I really like Mr. Scary as a character. It's nice having a dangerous SCP that doesn't actually want to be dangerous. Overall, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Is the rust supposed to be Mr. Scary and the guy be Mister Smiles?

I don't love the D just because he is self-aware, or something. But I liked this one as a whole. 1+.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: All previous Wondertainment articles have Mr. Scary. This is meant to be ambiguous, I think.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Shouldn't the name be "Mr.Smiles" in the catalog/scp-909-A because the slip of paper came from wondertainment?

item.title: I really like the character.
item.content:encoded: It's weird, very rarely do we ever find a skip that's a pretty okay guy, let alone a D-class, so when they do pop out like this, it's refreshing and makes it seem almost more real… as a further note it makes me feel really bad for Mr. Smile, something that's really hard to do, make someone feel bad for the skip. Hell, if I had clearance or authority I would request use of one of SCP-500 to be used on him to cure the rust. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My reading is that the Dr. W - Factory connection here is deliberately ambiguous.

The possibilities that immediately occur to me are:

  • Dr. W. was contaminated directly or indirectly by the Factory, but managed to stick the corruption into one of the Misters instead.
  • Dr. W. is the Factory, in one way or another. Altar ego, split personality, Jekell & Hyde, etc.
  • Mr. Smiley/Scary doesn't remember correctly because he's in the process of being corrupted by the Factory. The Factory trapped him here, and is gradually turning him from one to the other. The results may be unpleasant.
  • The Factory isn't really involved, this is just what Mr. Scary is supposed to do. Be scary.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Author can correct me if I am wrong, but this seems to be based off the "A Shift At The Factory" tale in which a man who worked at the Factory escaped and went on to become Dr. Wondertainment. In order to get rid of the rust disease inside him that he got from the Factory, he tried to transfer it to Mr. Smiles, but it appears doing that had unexpected consequences and thus this SCP formed.

Just my interpretation, but article is pretty good otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one reminded me a bit of the Labyrinth in Greek mythology.

The "father" building a labyrinth (though, it was Daedalus that built the Minotaur's, Minos ordered it) that's constantly changing and moving, a humanoid trapped in to keep away from other's.

I liked it because of the resemblance, although I don't know if it was your intention to make it similar to it, it was enough for my upvote.
Until I got to D-13321's investigation. He felt like an actual human being with emotions, and that was what gained you a +1 from me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's the link to SCP-455 that make me wonder.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked this.. The link between The Factory and DR.W really got me.

Quick nitpick: I noticed the Audio has the same collapsible name as the document (Document-2933-A) Dunno if that's a mistake or if I'm just not getting something.

Anyway, +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That the Mister isn't the primary contained object makes this a real standout as Misters go. I will admit I was disappointed to see that "Mr. Smiles" was real at the end; the "is he Wondertainment or Factory" question kept me really interested throughout the logs.

Also, your first sentence would sound better as "SCP-2933-1 is a steel structure partially submerged within a large saltwater reservoir." Also, I'm not sure about the use of 'corruption', that seems awfully vague for a scientific document. Upvoted regardless.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'd like to know how you made that audio file

[surreal sound enjoyment intensifies]

item.title: Re: Good work.
item.content:encoded: Agreed. Always nice to see a D-class with a personality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's sort of the reason to get specific there, though: technically rust is only for iron, but its also occasionally used as a general term for oxidation of metals. Something that was made of copper or chrome wouldn't oxidize in the way that they normally do, however, and form a protective shell of patina- they would corrode in the same way that iron compounds do. One of those "if you don't specify what "blue" means, somebody's going to get killed because they wore a cyan shirt" things.

item.title: Good work.
item.content:encoded:
At first I considered neutral vote because the article wasn't very original, but the exploration log and the things SCP-#-A said made me upvote.
But at the end of the day the thing that I really like was D-13321. That guy was pretty cool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Signs of rust/rusting" works just as good, as it specifies iron oxide corrosion.

No non-iron or non-iron alloy metal rusts, but they do undergo similar processes. Copper doesn't rust, it oxidies. Same(ish) process as iron, but we don't call it that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All of the Little Misters are Wondertainment products, so that's more or less required.

Can't explain off the factory bit, though. That's your prerogative.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting skip, good enough for me to upvote, but a touch more tinkering here and there wouldn't hurt. That bit with the Factory link is really what does it for me, story wise. Never really thought too much about Dr Wondertainment/The Factory interactions… there's several possibilities that moment could indicate.

One thing that stuck out:

signs of iron oxide corruption on their exterior surface

I think you might want something more like "signs of corrosion similar to iron oxide"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also going with a neutral vote. The Wondertainment aspect seems kinda forced into an otherwise bland article, but its written very well, especially the investigation log, and the audio file was creepy as dicks

one minor nitpicky thing: why are the designated "-1" and "-A" instead of 1&2 or A&B

EDIT: also not a huge fan of cramming both Dr. W and The Factory into an article that needs neither

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The majority of the article doesn't do much for me, but I'll be damned if that exploration log wasn't awesome. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No vote. It's okay but it feels like it's all been done before. The Wondertainment-as-neglectful-father-to-the-Misters was already done with the Tales of Mr. Collector, Wondertainment-is-the-Factory is general purpose headcanon (although I don't recall its specific use in an article), and the ever-changing-maze-as-contaiment has been done before as well.

It's a fine job at recycling older concepts, but it never becomes more than the sum of its parts.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting, this one.

+ show block
– hide block

The procedures and description are pretty thin, which makes sense given that they're basically an aperitif for the log. The shifting inescapable rust maze containment as a concept is a bit unremarkable, feeling like SCP-1864 without the effort or weirdness. I come down liking it, though, if only because I'm trying to wrap my head around what saltwater reservoir this thing could possibly be located in.

I don't quite buy the Factory/Wondertainment undernarrative. That is to say, you've mapped the Factory and Wondertainment onto a contagious anomaly and its containment, respectively. (The second being more Foundation territory, with parts of the description reading like smug approval.) The resulting narrative of this role switch-up is interesting, but (if I'm following correctly) the Wondertainment depicted here is a pretty huge stretch.

Also, creepy audio file didn't do it for me, but that's pretty standard for me.

Ultimately, though, +1 for D-13321. He was a real cool dude.

item.title: Dr. Wondertainment is a terrible father.
item.content:encoded:
I haven't written anything for the site since July, and even though I swore I would have this up on Halloween, I'm still a few hours late. So it goes. Regardless, this is my addition to the Little Misters series. I hope you enjoy it! Many thanks to those who checked it out in chat, specifically TwistedGears for his insights.

As always, your comments, critiques, and criticisms are appreciated.

Edit: Image is from https://www.needpix.com/photo/download/800233/abandoned-ocean-rusty-sea-ship-shipwreck-sky-water-waves and is in the public domain.
